title: US deep sea mining policy: The Metals Company's Craig Shesky
gist: A Benchmark Minerals Giga US 2026 interview listing featuring The Metals Company CFO Craig Shesky on US deep sea mining — arguing that permitting, regulation, and legal framework clarity are the primary gates to commercial development — alongside parallel sessions on real estate bottlenecks for US reindustrialisation, LFP manufacturing flexibility, and critical mineral supply chain investment.

themes.theme: Deep Sea Mining: Policy and Legal Framework as Commercial Gate

insights.claim: The Metals Company's Craig Shesky positions US deep sea mining commercial development as gated primarily by policy clarity on permitting, regulation, and the legal framework — not by technology limitations or resource availability.
insights.speaker: Craig Shesky
insights.so_what: Any US regulatory development on deep sea mining — permitting rules, legal framework definition, or international treaty interaction — is a direct catalyst or headwind for The Metals Company's commercial timeline.
insights.quote: US deep sea mining policy is the focus as Bryan Bille speaks with The Metals Company's Craig Shesky about permitting, regulation and the legal framework for deep sea mining
insights.locator: US deep sea mining policy: Interview with The Metals Company's Craig Shesky

insights.claim: Policy clarity on the legal framework for seabed mineral extraction is the threshold condition for commercial development of deep sea mining; without it, investment commitments cannot be made regardless of the underlying economics.
insights.speaker: Craig Shesky
insights.so_what: Investors watching The Metals Company (TMC) should treat US and international legal framework milestones as the key leading indicators rather than operational or geological progress.
insights.quote: policy clarity will be critical to commercial development
insights.locator: The interview explores why policy clarity will be critical to commercial development

insights.claim: The Metals Company's engagement with Benchmark Minerals at the Giga US 2026 conference reflects a policy-first development strategy — building regulator and policy community relationships before commercial scale-up, rather than waiting for rules to emerge.
insights.speaker: Craig Shesky
insights.so_what: Companies that actively shape the legal framework for deep sea mining are better positioned to obtain early permits when rules solidify; TMC's conference presence is partly a lobbying play.
insights.locator: US deep sea mining policy: Interview with The Metals Company's Craig Shesky

themes.theme: US Reindustrialisation Bottlenecks — Giga US 2026 Context

insights.claim: Land availability, power access, and permitting — not federal policy rhetoric — are identified by Savills' Ken Biberaj as the binding constraints on US reindustrialisation, requiring industrial policy to connect federal ambition with local execution.
insights.speaker: Ken Biberaj
insights.so_what: Industrial real estate with grid access is a scarce strategic input for US onshoring; scarcity of serviced industrial land is a durable investable theme across manufacturing and critical minerals.
insights.quote: US reindustrialisation depends not just on policy, but on the availability of land, power and permitting
insights.locator: Real estate and US reindustrialisation: Interview with Savills' Ken Biberaj

insights.claim: LG Energy Solution's strategic flexibility to shift manufacturing capacity toward LFP is cited at Giga US 2026 as a competitive advantage as US utility-scale energy storage demand accelerates, with policy certainty identified as the key prerequisite for further domestic investment.
insights.speaker: Bob Lee
insights.so_what: LFP manufacturing flexibility and IRA policy clarity are co-dependent; watch for LG Energy Solution capacity announcements following any regulatory signal on domestic content or storage incentives.
insights.quote: manufacturing flexibility is becoming a competitive advantage
insights.locator: US energy storage policy and manufacturing: Interview with LG Energy Solution's Bob Lee

insights.claim: Giga US 2026 framed critical mineral supply chain investment as essential to national security and economic competitiveness simultaneously, a dual-mandate framing that has historically unlocked bipartisan funding and long-term offtake structures.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Minerals
insights.so_what: Critical minerals retain bipartisan political insulation; the national security framing makes supply chain funding durable across administration changes — a structural positive for junior miners with US offtake exposure.
insights.quote: Accelerating investment in critical mineral supply chains crucial for economic competitiveness and national security
insights.locator: Investment Panel: Accelerating investment in critical mineral supply chains

source_meta.author: Craig Shesky, Chief Financial Officer, The Metals Company; Bryan Bille, Lead Policy and Geopolitical Analyst, Benchmark
